grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements rows 426, 429, 83, 384; taxonomy Administrative Law descriptions
grounding_note: Administrative Law routing depends entirely on WHICH agency and WHICH program — facts users summarize as 'the government cut my benefits.' Welfare (SNAP/TANF/DHS) vs SSI (Social Security) vs Medicaid vs PERS vs VA are distinct labels; federal vs state vs municipal general labels differ only by the agency's level. Users always know which office sent the letter.

scenarios.opening_query: My benefits got cut off last month and nobody at the office will explain anything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Welfare || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my SSI from the Social Security office, not the state assistance.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my SSI — the Social Security office, not the state people.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which agency and program stopped the benefit
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my SNAP food benefits through the state DHS office.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my SNAP food benefits from the state DHS office.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'My benefits' is the canonical ambiguous phrase; program identity is a pure routing disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: The state cut our family's assistance and we can't feed four kids on what's left.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Welfare || Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid
scenarios.hidden_fact: What they actually terminated is the kids' OHP health coverage — the food benefits are fine.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the kids' OHP health coverage they ended — food benefits are fine.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the TANF cash assistance they cut, over a work-requirement dispute.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the TANF cash they cut — some work-requirement dispute.
scenarios.vetting_note: OHP is the taxonomy's named Medicaid example; families bundle all programs as 'assistance.'

scenarios.opening_query: I'm a retired public employee and there's a serious problem with my retirement money.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > PERS || Family Law > QDROs
scenarios.hidden_fact: The problem comes from my divorce — the decree divides my PERS with my ex, and the division order was never done right.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It traces to my divorce — the decree splits my PERS with my ex, and the division order was botched.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the retirement dispute stems from a divorce decree or the plan itself
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No divorce involved — PERS itself miscalculated my service years and cut my monthly amount.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No divorce — PERS miscalculated my service years and cut my checks.
scenarios.vetting_note: The taxonomy's own PERS/QDRO distinction verbatim; source-of-dispute is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: The VA is giving my husband the runaround and we've hit a wall dealing with them.
scenarios.final: Real Property > Government Loans (VA, FHA, etc.)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Military/Veterans || Real Property > Government Loans (VA, FHA, etc.)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's about our VA home loan — the lender says the VA guarantee paperwork is defective and closing is frozen.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the VA home loan — the guarantee paperwork is supposedly defective and our closing is frozen.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the VA issue is benefits/healthcare or a VA-backed home loan
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's his disability rating — they denied the increase despite his new diagnosis.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's his disability rating — they denied the increase despite the new diagnosis.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same agency, two distant labels; 'the VA' hides whether it's benefits or housing finance (rows 384/424 pattern).

scenarios.opening_query: The school is failing my son and I've exhausted every meeting and email I can think of.
scenarios.initial: Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education || Administrative Law > Student Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: He was suspended for an article he wrote in the student paper criticizing the principal — this is about his speech rights, not services.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They suspended him over an article he wrote criticizing the principal — it's about his speech rights.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute concerns disability services or civil liberties
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He has an IEP for autism and the school has ignored its requirements all year.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He has an autism IEP the school has ignored all year.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 429's boundary: IEP failure vs civil-liberties discipline; 'school is failing him' covers both.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter keeps getting in trouble at school and I think it's connected to her struggles.
scenarios.final: Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Student Rights || Administrative Law > Schools — Special Needs Education
scenarios.hidden_fact: She has diagnosed ADHD and dyslexia, and the school refuses to evaluate her for an IEP — the 'trouble' is them disciplining her disability.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She has diagnosed ADHD and dyslexia and they refuse to evaluate her for an IEP — they're disciplining her disability.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a disability underlies the discipline and services were denied
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No disability involved — they searched her phone and locker without cause and suspended her without any hearing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No disability — they searched her phone and locker without cause and suspended her with no hearing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 429 verbatim ('past experiences affecting mental health, trouble at school'); diagnosis fact selects the IEP route.

scenarios.opening_query: A government office rejected my paperwork for the third time and I'm losing my mind.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > General (State) || Administrative Law > General (Federal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the FAA — they keep rejecting my experimental aircraft registration on contradictory grounds.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the FAA — my experimental aircraft registration, rejected on contradictory grounds each time.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which level of government agency is involved
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the state elections division rejecting my ballot-initiative petition formatting.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the state elections division — they keep rejecting my initiative petition's formatting.
scenarios.vetting_note: Agency level is the sole difference between the general admin labels; both examples come from the descriptions.

scenarios.opening_query: My professional life is being destroyed by an accusation at work that isn't true.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > General - Employee || Administrative Law > Professional Licensing
scenarios.hidden_fact: The nursing board opened a license investigation over the accusation — that's the part that can end my career, beyond the job itself.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The nursing board opened a license investigation — that's what can end my career, not just this job.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a licensing board proceeding is underway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No board involvement — it's an internal HR investigation and a suspension I'm disputing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No board — it's internal HR and a suspension I'm fighting.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rows 83/420: board proceedings are a separate legal system from employment; the investigation fact reroutes.

scenarios.opening_query: My contractor's license got suspended and I can't work. This is how I feed my family.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Professional Licensing || Criminal Law > DUII/DWS
scenarios.hidden_fact: The suspension is automatic fallout from my pending DUII case — fixing the criminal case is what restores the license.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's automatic fallout from my pending DUII — resolving the criminal case is what gets the license back.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the license action stems from a criminal case
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a board discipline case over a customer complaint — no criminal charges anywhere.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's board discipline over a customer complaint — nothing criminal.
scenarios.vetting_note: Downstream-vs-independent license action; the criminal-case fact redirects the whole matter.

scenarios.opening_query: Our small counseling practice got a letter terminating our biggest contract without cause and it will sink us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Administrative Law > Medicare/Medicaid
scenarios.hidden_fact: The contract is with the state's Medicaid coordinated-care organization — 235 of our clients are covered through it, so this is a Medicaid provider-network fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The contract is with the state Medicaid CCO — 235 clients are covered through it, so it's a Medicaid provider fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the terminated contract is with a government health program
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a private employee-assistance-program contract with a national insurer — no government program involved.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a private EAP contract with a national insurer — no government program.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 426 verbatim (CareOregon termination); the humans split on exactly this program-identity fact.

scenarios.opening_query: The city denied my application and gave me two sentences of explanation after eight months of waiting.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal) || Real Property > Land Use/Zoning
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a variance application — I want to build an accessory dwelling my lot's zoning doesn't allow, and the planning commission said no.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a zoning variance — I want to build an ADU my lot's zoning doesn't allow, and planning said no.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the denied application concerns land use and zoning
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my sidewalk-vendor permit for the food cart — nothing to do with property or building.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my food-cart vendor permit — nothing to do with property.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'City denied my application' spans generic municipal disputes and the dedicated zoning label.

scenarios.opening_query: My unemployment claim was denied and I have a hearing scheduled in three weeks. I need representation.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Unemployment || Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: At the fact-finding, my employer claimed 'misconduct' — but the real story is they fired me for filing a workers' comp claim, and I want to go after them for that firing too, beyond the hearing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The employer claims 'misconduct,' but they really fired me for my workers' comp claim — I want to pursue the firing itself, not just the hearing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user seeks only the benefits hearing or also an unlawful-termination claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I just need someone at the hearing — the firing itself was a legitimate layoff; the state's reasoning is what's wrong.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I just need help winning the hearing — the layoff itself was legit; the state's reasoning is what's wrong.
scenarios.vetting_note: Replaces v1's role-reversed unemployment flips with a relief-sought disclosure: hearing-only vs hearing-plus-retaliation-claim.

scenarios.opening_query: I was let go unfairly and now I have no income coming in at all. I need legal help this week.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Administrative Law > Unemployment
scenarios.hidden_fact: The urgent piece is my unemployment denial — my appeal hearing with the state is Tuesday and I can't do it alone.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The urgent thing is the unemployment denial — my state appeal hearing is Tuesday.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the immediate need is the state benefits hearing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Unemployment came through fine — what I want is to sue over the firing, which happened the day after I testified for a coworker.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Unemployment came through — I want to sue over the firing, which came right after I testified for a coworker.
scenarios.vetting_note: Mirror of 12: same grievance, immediate-relief fact selects agency hearing vs employment suit (paper's employment_admin failure mode).

scenarios.opening_query: The state says I was overpaid benefits during the pandemic and now they want $18,000 back.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Unemployment || Administrative Law > Welfare
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's not unemployment — the demand is from DHS for SNAP and childcare assistance they now claim I didn't qualify for.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's DHS — SNAP and childcare assistance they now say I didn't qualify for.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which program's overpayment is being demanded
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the unemployment office — they reclassified my pandemic claims and want the benefits back.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the unemployment office — they reclassified my pandemic claims.
scenarios.vetting_note: Overpayment demands look identical across programs; the issuing agency is the only routing fact.
